title: Decorative Wood Sleigh
summary: A small decorative sleigh traced from a printed pattern onto 1x12 sides, with a beveled front and back — a quick holiday or year-round shelf-decor build.
description:
This sleigh's shape comes entirely from a printable pattern rather than measured cuts — print the diagram at actual size, trace it onto the 1x12 side boards with a ballpoint pen pressed firmly (no need to cut the paper out first, just transfer the outline as an indent), then cut both sides to the traced line. Since the two sides are traced from the same pattern rather than measured independently, they naturally match without any extra fitting work.

It's worth painting the pieces before assembly rather than after, since a small, curved piece like this is far easier to finish flat on a bench than once it's a fully enclosed box shape. Attach the bottom flush to the first opening on the sleigh's curved legs, then fit the back and front pieces in, nailing and gluing each — the back and front both get their bottom edges beveled (30° and 15° respectively) so they sit flush against the sleigh's curved profile rather than leaving a gap.

**A wider board gives a wider sleigh** — the whole design scales directly with the width of stock you start with, so this is a genuinely easy project to size up or down without redrawing anything. If you don't have a miter saw for the beveled edges, cutting them square instead is a perfectly reasonable substitution — the difference only shows on the inside of the piece, never from the outside.

tools.note: Cutting the traced sleigh side pattern.
The beveled front/back edges; square cuts work as a substitute.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Traced from a printed pattern, one side used to check the other for matching.
Longest point, bottom edge cut at 30° bevel.
Longest point, bottom edge cut at 15° bevel.

steps.title: Trace and cut the sides
steps.body: Print the sleigh pattern at actual size and trace onto the 1x12 board with a ballpoint pen, pressing firmly to leave an indent. Cut both sides with a jigsaw, using the first as reference to check the second matches.

steps.title: Paint the pieces
steps.body: It's easier to paint or stain the individual pieces now, while they're still flat, rather than after assembly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the bottom flush to the first opening on the sleigh side legs, using screws or nails and glue.

steps.title: Fit the back and front
steps.body: Cut the back and front pieces with their bottom edges beveled (30° for the back, 15° for the front) so they sit flush against the sleigh's curve. Fit and nail and glue each in place.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand any rough edges and touch up the finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
